home.name: Home / Interior Design
home.slide_concept: Same room, same angle, different interior styles

slide_structure.1_hook: Show the ugly/dated room — the relatable problem. Scene-only, no person needed.
slide_structure.2_problem: Amplify the pain — close-up of clashing furniture, messy corner, outdated decor.
slide_structure.3_discovery: Person discovers the app/product — opens it on their phone, first reaction.
slide_structure.4_transform_1: Same room, first style transformation (e.g., Mid-century modern). 'Wait this actually looks good?'
slide_structure.5_transform_2: Same room, second style transformation (e.g., Japandi). 'Okay I'm obsessed.'
slide_structure.6_cta: Person smiling confidently. App name + link in bio energy.

hook_formulas: I showed my mum what AI thinks our [room] should look like
My landlord said I can't change anything so I showed her this
POV: You have good taste but no budget
IKEA budget, designer taste

beauty.name: Beauty / Cosmetics
beauty.slide_concept: Same face, same angle, progressive enhancement or problem-to-solution story

slide_structure.1_hook: The relatable skin/beauty problem. Person looking frustrated. Selfie or mirror selfie.
slide_structure.2_problem: Amplify the struggle — failed attempts, cakey makeup, dull skin close-up.
slide_structure.3_discovery: Person discovers the product — unboxing, holding it up, first impression.
slide_structure.4_transform_1: Applying the product — first results visible. 'Wait this actually works?'
slide_structure.5_transform_2: Full glow-up — radiant skin, confident expression. 'Okay I'm obsessed.'
slide_structure.6_cta: Person smiling confidently, glowing skin. Product name + link in bio.

prompt_rules.preservation: Face must be PIXEL-PERFECT identical across slides. NO skin smoothing. ONLY change the target feature.

hook_formulas: Everyone keeps asking if I got [treatment] done
I've always had [feature] and never known what [change] would look like
Stop wishing for better skin — I actually found something that works
My [person] got [treatment] and now I can't stop thinking about it

fitness.name: Fitness / Body
fitness.slide_concept: Same person, same pose, progressive transformation

slide_structure.1_hook: The relatable fitness frustration. Mirror selfie or gym photo showing 'before' state.
slide_structure.2_problem: Amplify — failed diets, gym confusion, no progress despite effort.
slide_structure.3_discovery: Person discovers the product/app/program. Holding it up or showing on phone.
slide_structure.4_transform_1: Month 1 results — subtle but visible changes. 'Wait is this working?'
slide_structure.5_transform_2: Month 3+ results — clear transformation. 'Okay this actually changed everything.'
slide_structure.6_cta: Confident pose. Product/app name + link in bio.

hook_formulas: My trainer said I'd never look like [goal]
I showed my gym buddy what AI thinks I could look like in 6 months
3 months apart. Same mirror, same angle.
POV: You actually stuck with it this time

saas.name: Productivity / SaaS
saas.slide_concept: Before/after workflow visualization or results demonstration

slide_structure.1_hook: The messy 'before' — chaotic spreadsheets, cluttered desktop, overwhelming task list.
slide_structure.2_problem: Amplify — person stressed, wasting time, missed deadlines.
slide_structure.3_discovery: Person discovers the app. Phone/laptop screen showing clean UI.
slide_structure.4_transform_1: The workflow now — organized, automated, clean dashboard.
slide_structure.5_transform_2: The results — time saved, goals hit, relaxed and productive.
slide_structure.6_cta: Person smiling at laptop/phone. App name + link in bio.

hook_formulas: My boss said my workflow is a mess so I showed her this
I was spending 4 hours on [task] until I found this
POV: You automate the one thing that was eating your day
This app saved me [X] hours a week — not exaggerating

food.name: Food / Recipe
food.slide_concept: Same ingredients, different meals OR same dish, progressive cooking steps

slide_structure.1_hook: The relatable food struggle — boring meals, empty fridge, takeout guilt.
slide_structure.2_problem: Amplify — same 3 meals on repeat, unhealthy habits, wasted groceries.
slide_structure.3_discovery: Person discovers the product/app/cookbook. Holding it or showing on phone.
slide_structure.4_transform_1: First meal made — plated beautifully. 'Wait I actually made this?'
slide_structure.5_transform_2: Second meal or meal prep — variety and quality. 'Okay I'm a chef now.'
slide_structure.6_cta: Person enjoying food, smiling. Product name + link in bio.

hook_formulas: I eat the same 3 meals every week and I'm done with it
This turned my sad desk lunch into THIS
POV: You actually meal prep and it doesn't taste like cardboard
My grocery bill went down but my meals went UP
